label: GROUP A: ILO ILOSTAT
context: 12 indicators | DEPS submits national LFS to ilostat.ilo.org annually (ILO member since 1993) | Annual KILM + SDMX submissions | Brunei actively participating | Key: ISCO-08 coding accuracy & timely LFS submission | Conference Board TED (5.1.4) = easy fix

indicators.name: Labour Rights
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — ILCSTAT Labour Rights Index
indicators.mechanism: ILO internal model using country reports, convention ratifications, labour law compliance & court records
indicators.submission: Countries do NOT submit directly. ILO researchers collect from national laws, ILO country reports filed under Convention monitoring, court records & media analysis. Brunei submits periodic reports to ILO supervisory bodies.
indicators.included: YES — Brunei is ILO member since 1993; data automatically compiled
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE. Brunei is an ILO member state. Improvement: ratify additional ILO Core Conventions not yet ratified (check normlex.ilo.org); strengthen enforcement; submit comprehensive annual country report.
indicators.action: Ratify ILO Conventions No.87 (Freedom of Association) & No.98 (Collective Bargaining); strengthen Labour Department enforcement; engage ILO Regional Office Bangkok (ro-bangkok@ilo.org); submit detailed periodic reports under ratified conventions.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / Labour Department
indicators.contact: ILO Regional Office Asia-Pacific: ro-bangkok@ilo.org | ILO NORMLEX: normlex.ilo.org | Jakarta office: jakarta@ilo.org
indicators.insight: Rank 83. Brunei has ratified only 15 of 190 ILO Conventions. Ratifying Core Conventions No.87 and No.98 would signal commitment and improve ILO compliance scores. Key gap: freedom of association and collective bargaining frameworks.

indicators.name: Gender Parity in High-Skilled Jobs
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey (LFS) microdata
indicators.mechanism: DEPS national LFS; ILO derives gender parity ratios from employment-by-occupation ISCO-08 classification submitted to ILOSTAT
indicators.submission: DEPS submits annual LFS microdata or tabulations to ILOSTAT via ilostat.ilo.org portal; annual KILM submission; SDMX data exchange protocol
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS to ILOSTAT regularly
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure ISCO-08 occupation coding is applied correctly in LFS to capture professionals (ISCO major group 2) vs associate professionals (group 3) by gender accurately.
indicators.action: DEPS to validate ISCO-08 gender-by-occupation cross-tabs in LFS; submit annually to ilostat@ilo.org; promote women into STEM and professional roles through MOE and MoFE gender programmes.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | ILOSTAT portal: ilostat.ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 48 — genuine strength. Women = 56% of Brunei professional/technical workforce. Maintain annual LFS ISCO-08 submission to ILOSTAT. Outperforms Singapore on this indicator.

indicators.name: Youth Inclusion (NEET Rate)
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey microdata
indicators.mechanism: DEPS LFS captures youth 15–24 by employment, education and training status; ILO computes NEET rate from DEPS annual submissions
indicators.submission: DEPS submits annual LFS microdata/tabulations to ILOSTAT; annual KILM; SDMX exchange. LFS 2025 must be submitted within 3 months of completion.
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS annually to ILOSTAT
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure LFS 2025 results submitted urgently. Youth NEET coding must follow ILO 19th ICLS definitions. MOE school enrolment data should cross-validate.
indicators.action: DEPS to submit LFS 2025 to ilostat@ilo.org within 3 months of fieldwork completion; MOE-DEPS joint validation of youth NEET coding; MOE Transition Year Programme + IBTE 6-month fast-track certificates to reduce NEET structurally.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | JCB: jobcentrebrunei.gov.bn | PPTM: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 66, improving. JCB 2024: 3,299 local private sector hires; unemployment fell to 4.8%. LFS 2025 submission is the immediate priority. Structural: MOE Transition Year Programme + IBTE fast-track certificates target NEET reduction.

indicators.name: Vulnerable Employment
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey microdata
indicators.mechanism: ILO computes % own-account + contributing family workers (inverse = formal employment) from DEPS LFS ISCO classifications
indicators.submission: DEPS submits LFS annual tabulations to ILOSTAT; SDMX data exchange via ilostat.ilo.org
indicators.included: YES — World rank 10. Strong performance already reported
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. World Rank 10 — exceptional. Data pipeline functioning correctly. Maintain annual LFS submission to ILOSTAT.
indicators.action: MAINTAIN: ensure LFS annual submission to ILOSTAT on time. Minor improvement possible as O&G sector formalises remaining contract workers. No structural change urgently needed.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: World #10 — exceptional strength. Near-universal formal employment driven by large public sector and O&G workforce. Maintain ILOSTAT annual LFS submission. Strong Brunei competitive advantage.

indicators.name: Workforce with Secondary Education
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey (LFS) microdata
indicators.mechanism: DEPS LFS captures highest educational attainment of employed persons; ILO derives % with at least secondary education (ISCED 2+) from annual submission
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission to ILOSTAT with ISCED-based education coding; annual KILM submission
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS. Score declining -10.6 pts — urgent investigation needed
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. INVESTIGATE: -10.6 pt decline may reflect (a) positive compositional shift as tertiary graduates replace secondary-only workers, or (b) IBTE graduates miscoded as below-secondary in LFS. Fix coding before next submission.
indicators.action: DEPS-MOE joint audit of LFS education coding; verify IBTE Diploma graduates are coded as ISCED Level 3 TVET (NOT below-secondary); submit corrected data to ILOSTAT; adult secondary completion programme (500/year).
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | MOE: moe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 28 — good overall but declining -10.6 pts. Most likely positive: tertiary workers replacing secondary-only. CRITICAL: verify IBTE Diploma coded as ISCED 3 in LFS — miscoding as 'below secondary' artificially suppresses score.

indicators.name: Population with Secondary Education
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Population Census / LFS
indicators.mechanism: ILO derives population educational attainment from DEPS Population Census 2021 and LFS education module; ISCED 2011 classification applied
indicators.submission: DEPS submits Population Census 2021 education attainment tables and LFS to ILOSTAT; next census 2031
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits census/LFS data to ILOSTAT
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure Census 2021 education attainment tables submitted to ILOSTAT in full. MOE secondary completion rates feed long-term trend upward.
indicators.action: DEPS to submit Census 2021 education attainment tables to ILOSTAT; MOE to publish annual secondary completion rate; ensure all IBTE TVET completers captured in census education coding.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 27 — strong. Singapore ranks 6 (83.1). Gap reflects Brunei's younger population mix and historical secondary completion rates. Long-term improvement as IBTE/MOE secondary expansion reaches working-age population over 5–10 years.

indicators.name: Technicians & Associate Professionals
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT + World Bank Global Jobs Indicators Database (JOIN)
indicators.mechanism: ILO computes % employed in ISCO-08 major group 3 (Technicians & Associate Professionals) from DEPS LFS; cross-referenced with World Bank JOIN database
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission with ISCO-08 occupation coding to ILOSTAT; also submitted to World Bank JOIN (data.worldbank.org)
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS. Score improving +6.94 pts
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure ISCO-08 major group 3 is correctly coded in LFS. IBTE expansion will grow this indicator long-term. Currently OUTPERFORMS Singapore (57.06 vs 52.43).
indicators.action: DEPS to validate ISCO-08 major group 3 coding for IBTE TVET graduates; submit LFS annually to ILOSTAT and World Bank JOIN; expand IBTE technical programmes to sustain and improve this score.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | World Bank JOIN: data.worldbank.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Brunei OUTPERFORMS Singapore (rank 36 vs 42). IBTE expansion will grow this further. Maintain ISCO-08 coding quality in LFS submissions. One of Brunei's genuine strengths.

indicators.name: Educated Unemployment
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey microdata
indicators.mechanism: ILO computes unemployment rate among tertiary-educated persons from DEPS LFS education × employment cross-tabulation; inverse = this score
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission to ILOSTAT with education-by-employment cross-tabs; ISCED coding required
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS annually to ILOSTAT
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure LFS education-employment cross-tabs submitted to ILOSTAT annually. Score 83.21 reflects low graduate unemployment — a genuine strength.
indicators.action: DEPS to ensure education × employment cross-tabs in LFS submitted to ILOSTAT; MOE annual Graduate Tracer Study cross-validates; JCB graduate placement maintains low educated unemployment.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | JCB: jobcentrebrunei.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 48 — good. Low educated unemployment reflects Brunei's economy where government and O&G absorb most graduates. Long-term: diversify employment base as graduate output grows to avoid future educated unemployment.

indicators.name: Workforce with Tertiary Education
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey microdata
indicators.mechanism: ILO derives % employed persons with tertiary education (ISCED 5–8) from DEPS LFS education module; annual KILM indicator
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission with ISCED 2011 education coding to ILOSTAT portal
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS. Score improving +7.86 pts
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure ISCED 5–8 coding correct for all tertiary-educated returning Bruneians in LFS. Score grows naturally as scholarship cohorts enter workforce over 5–7 years.
indicators.action: DEPS to validate ISCED 5–8 coding in LFS; ensure overseas graduates captured in LFS when they return to Brunei workforce; MOE overseas student expansion (3.1.2) will feed this indicator in 5–7 years.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 62, improving +7.86 pts. Structural ceiling: score grows as more MOE government scholarship cohorts complete degrees and return to workforce. Ensure DEPS captures returning overseas graduates in LFS education coding.

indicators.name: Professionals (% of Workforce)
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT + World Bank Global Jobs Indicators Database (JOIN)
indicators.mechanism: ILO computes % employed in ISCO-08 major group 2 (Professionals) from DEPS LFS; cross-referenced with World Bank JOIN
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission to ILOSTAT; also to World Bank JOIN database (data.worldbank.org)
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS to ILOSTAT and World Bank JOIN
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Ensure ISCO-08 major group 2 correctly coded in LFS. Growing professional workforce through tertiary education expansion drives improvement long-term.
indicators.action: DEPS to validate ISCO-08 major group 2 coding; tertiary education expansion (3.1.2) drives long-term professional workforce growth; ensure returning overseas graduates correctly coded as professionals.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 45. Slight decline -0.70. Professional workforce grows as tertiary graduates enter jobs. Long-term fix: increase tertiary GER and reduce skills mismatch (5.2.1). ISCO-08 coding accuracy is critical.

indicators.name: Senior Officials and Managers
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT + World Bank Global Jobs Indicators Database (JOIN)
indicators.mechanism: ILO computes % employed in ISCO-08 major group 1 (Managers, Senior Officials & Legislators) from DEPS LFS
indicators.submission: DEPS annual LFS submission to ILOSTAT and World Bank JOIN; ISCO-08 major group 1 coding required
indicators.included: YES — DEPS submits LFS. WORLD RANK 14 — exceptional performance
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. World Rank 14 globally — exceptional management talent proportion. MAINTAIN through LFS accuracy and continued senior management development.
indicators.action: MAINTAIN: DEPS continues annual LFS submission with ISCO-08 major group 1 coding; include both public and private sector managers; leverage this rank in all GTCI marketing materials.
indicators.contact: ilostat@ilo.org | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | CSC: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: WORLD RANK 14 — exceptional. Reflects Brunei's large public sector + O&G managerial workforce. IPA and CSC management development programmes sustain this. Use in all talent attraction marketing.

indicators.name: Skills Matching
indicators.provider: ILO ILOSTAT — Labour Force Survey microdata (Workers whose education matches their occupation)
indicators.mechanism: EOS question on how well skills of workers match available jobs
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB NPI; declining -1.11 pts
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Declining trend. Improving requires better labour market intelligence (LMI), JCB matching efficiency, and IBTE/MOE curriculum alignment to actual job demand.
indicators.action: PPTM to publish annual National Skills Demand Assessment; JCB to improve digital job matching platform; MOE to implement Critical Shortage Occupation List; UTB to brief EOS respondents on JCB improvements and skills matching initiatives.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | JCB: jobcentrebrunei.gov.bn | PPTM: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: ⚠️ SOURCE CORRECTED: This indicator belongs to GROUP A (ILOSTAT), NOT Group B (WEF EOS). Rank 59, declining -1.11. JCB job matching improvements and Annual National Skills Demand Assessment will help. UTB EOS respondent briefing on JCB digital platform improvements and PPTM skills data can shift perceptions within 12–18 months.

label: GROUP B: WEF Executive Opinion Survey
context: 15 indicators | ~100 Brunei executives surveyed annually via UTB (Universiti Teknologi Brunei) as National Partner Institute (NPI) | Online questionnaire Jan–Mar | UTB respondent briefing quality is the single most impactful lever for ALL 17 EOS indicators | Contact: eos@weforum.org

indicators.name: Extent of Market Dominance
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: Annual survey of ~14,000 business executives in 135+ countries; Brunei ~100 executives answer perception questions on market competition (scale 1–7); managed by UTB (Universiti Teknologi Brunei) as National Partner Institute (NPI)
indicators.submission: WEF sends online questionnaire to UTB (Jan–Mar each year); UTB recruits and manages ~100 Brunei executive respondents; responses submitted online via WEF EOS portal (eos@weforum.org)
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB as National Partner Institute
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Key issue: respondent quality and briefing. ~100 executives drive this score. Diversifying pool beyond SOE executives + briefing on actual competition reforms (Competition Act under review) would shift perceptions.
indicators.action: UTB to diversify EOS respondent pool to include SME executives and private sector; brief respondents on actual market competition reforms; develop annual Brunei EOS Briefing Pack; accelerate BEDB Invest Brunei economic diversification; Competition Act implementation.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | WEF Centre New Economy: weforum.org/centres/centre-for-new-economy-and-society
indicators.insight: Rank 99 — weak. Market concentration is REAL (O&G + royal-linked conglomerates dominate). Fix requires BOTH actual competition policy reform AND better EOS respondent briefing by UTB. UTB is the key lever as NPI.

indicators.name: Cluster Development
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on whether well-developed business clusters (geographic concentrations of interconnected companies) exist in the country
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online questionnaire to ~100 Brunei business executives (Jan–Mar)
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB as NPI
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Improvement requires: (1) actual cluster development (Pulau Muara Besar, BIBD Fintech Hub, Halal Industry cluster), AND (2) briefing EOS respondents on existing cluster developments.
indicators.action: UTB to develop cluster communications narrative; brief EOS respondents on PMB industrial zone, Brunei Halal Industry cluster, UTB Technology Park; communicate BEDB's cluster development programme in economic media.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 82. Brunei lacks well-developed industrial clusters (dominated by O&G). BEDB's PMB petrochemicals cluster and Halal Industry zone are the core narratives. Communicate progress to EOS respondents via an annual UTB EOS Briefing Pack.

indicators.name: Labour-Employer Cooperation
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on quality of labour-employer relations and cooperation in the workplace (scale 1–7)
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online questionnaire to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB NPI; score improved +9.33 pts
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Positive improvement trend (+9.33). Continue through institutionalised bipartite dialogue, transparent industrial relations framework, and positive communication of cooperation outcomes.
indicators.action: UTB to brief EOS respondents on National Labour Council bipartite dialogue outcomes; Labour Department to promote HR excellence awards; communicate Brunei's non-confrontational labour culture and zero industrial action record.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / Labour Department / UTB
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | Labour Dept: labour.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Score improved +9.33 — positive momentum. Brunei's non-confrontational labour culture is a genuine strength. UTB respondent education on National Labour Council outcomes and cooperative HR practices is the key lever.

indicators.name: Professional Management
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on whether management positions are held by professional managers based on merit vs political/personal connections
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Score improved massively +18.19 pts (42.44 → 60.63)
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. MAJOR improvement +18.19 pts signals shift toward perceived meritocracy. Continue by promoting civil service performance management, transparency in corporate governance, merit-based private sector HR.
indicators.action: UTB to brief EOS respondents on Public Service Excellence Framework and private sector HR reforms; communicate merit-based initiatives (GCC performance pay, DE HR excellence awards); continue CSC management reforms.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Finance & Economy / CSC / UTB
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | CSC: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: MASSIVE improvement +18.19 pts. Positive momentum must be sustained through continued UTB respondent briefings on Public Sector Performance Management Framework, GCC reforms, and DE private sector HR excellence programmes.

indicators.name: Relationship of Pay-Productivity
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on whether pay is linked to worker productivity and performance (scale 1–7)
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Score DECLINING -8.66 pts. Pay-performance link perceived as weakening
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING BUT DECLINING. Score worsening -8.66 reflects perception that Brunei salaries not tied to productivity (civil service seniority-based pay, O&G fixed wages). Requires actual HR reform AND EOS respondent communication.
indicators.action: MoFE to introduce performance-linked pay legislation for private sector; CSC to expand pay-for-performance bands in civil service; UTB to communicate private sector HR reforms to EOS respondents; DE HR Excellence Awards.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | Labour Dept: labour.gov.bn
indicators.insight: DECLINING -8.66 — concern. Reflects perception that salaries not tied to productivity. Requires actual HR reform (performance pay legislation) AND communication of changes to EOS respondents. Two-track fix needed.

indicators.name: Brain Gain
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS question on country's ability to attract talented people from abroad; perception-based (scale 1–7)
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — RANK 16 globally — exceptional performance
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Rank 16 — a major strength. Reflects perception of good living conditions (zero income tax, safety, healthcare, political stability). MAINTAIN and improve by expanding foreign talent visa pathways.
indicators.action: MAINTAIN: UTB to brief EOS respondents on Global Talent Pass initiatives; MoHA to streamline highly skilled foreign worker passes; communicate Brunei quality-of-life advantages (zero income tax, safety, free healthcare) prominently.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / UTB / Labour Dept
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | Immigration: immigration.gov.bn
indicators.insight: World RANK 16 — exceptional. Tax-free income, political stability, safety, and quality of life drive positive perceptions. MAINTAIN by streamlining talent visa, promoting Brunei as ASEAN HQ location, and briefing EOS respondents on talent-friendly policies.

indicators.name: Social Mobility
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on equality of opportunity and social mobility — can people from any background succeed through merit?
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives

indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Exceptional improvement +24.32 — one of Brunei's best GTCI gains this cycle. Continue through: government scholarship accessibility, private sector merit hiring, and communicating social mobility successes.
indicators.action: UTB to brief EOS respondents on MOE government scholarship accessibility, DE SME support; MOE to publish social mobility data (scholarship uptake by background); continue Wawasan 2035 meritocracy narrative in public communications.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | MOE Scholarship Mgmt: hiedbrunei.moe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: EXCEPTIONAL improvement +24.32 pts. Indicates growing perception of meritocracy. UTB EOS respondent briefing is critical to sustain momentum. Communicate MOE government scholarship accessibility and DE SME support stories prominently.

indicators.name: Leadership Opportunities for Women
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025

indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Rank 28 globally; strong improvement +18.30 pts

indicators.agency: Ministry of Culture, Youth & Sports / UTB / MoFE
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 28 globally. +18.30 pts improvement shows growing recognition of women in leadership. Continue through UTB respondent briefings and actual female executive appointment targets in public sector GLCs.

indicators.name: Employee Development
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on extent to which companies invest in employee training and professional development
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB NPI

indicators.name: Delegation of Authority

indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives

indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | IPA: ipa.gov.bn

indicators.name: Social Protection

indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB NPI. Score declining -5.97 pts
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. Declining. Requires: (1) actual social protection enhancement (SCP, TAP, healthcare access), AND (2) communication of Brunei's social safety net to EOS respondents who likely underestimate it. Also advocate with WEF to use most recent EOS data.
indicators.action: UTB to brief EOS respondents on TAP (100% coverage), Supplementary Contributory Pension (SCP), free government healthcare, BSB housing schemes; MoFE to enhance social protection floor; advocate WEF use 2025 EOS data for this indicator.

indicators.name: Brain Retention
indicators.provider: WEF Executive Opinion Survey (EOS) 2025
indicators.mechanism: EOS perception question on whether talented Bruneians tend to leave the country or pursue careers domestically
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Score improving +5.43 pts but rank 61

indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | MOE: moe.gov.bn | JCB: jobcentrebrunei.gov.bn | PPTM: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: RANK 119/135 — BRUNEI'S SINGLE WORST GTCI INDICATOR. Genuine structural crisis. Skills mismatch between education output and employer needs is acute. Requires BOTH actual reforms (curriculum, IABs, internships) AND UTB respondent education. Manpower Blueprint 2024 is the anchor document.

indicators.action: MOE Department of Scholarship Management to compile ALL overseas Brunei students (MOE government scholars ~4,000–5,000; government-funded non-bonded ~500–1,000; self-funded ~1,500–2,500); submit as 'outbound internationally mobile students' in UNESCO Annual Education Survey by 31 March 2026.
indicators.agency: MOE (Higher Education Division / Department of Scholarship Management)

World Bank FUNDS the survey. One request resolves BOTH 1.3.7 (Firms with Website) AND 3.2.2 (Training in Firms) simultaneously.
indicators.action: MoFE to write formal request letter to enterprisesurveys@worldbank.org requesting Brunei WBES 2025/2026 inclusion; MOE to provide supporting letter on training supply infrastructure; facilitate World Bank field researcher access to Brunei firms; estimated survey cost: World Bank funded.

indicators.action: MoFE to request WBES inclusion (resolves 1.3.7 AND 3.2.2 simultaneously); IBTE to expand corporate training MOUs; SkillsPlus scheme expansion; advocate for Skills Development Levy (0.5% payroll) to structurally increase training prevalence.

indicators.name: Women Economic Empowerment

indicators.action: AGC to review Brunei legislation against all 8 WBL dimensions; engage World Bank WBL team (wbl@worldbank.org) for methodology consultation; Ministry of Culture to propose gender equity legislation aligned with Wawasan 2035; targeted legal reform on WBL components with lowest scores.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Culture, Youth & Sports / AGC / MoFE
indicators.contact: World Bank WBL: wbl@worldbank.org | WBL portal: wbl.worldbank.org | AGC: agc.gov.bn
indicators.insight: RANK 126 — critical structural weakness. WBL is based on ACTUAL LAWS — no data submission shortcut. AGC must conduct WBL component-by-component legislative gap analysis. Each WBL dimension addressed = immediate score improvement. Long-term structural fix.

indicators.contact: World Bank Entrepreneurship DB portal: worldbank.org/en/programs/entrepreneurship | Sources page: worldbank.org/en/programs/entrepreneurship/sources | ROCBN: info.rocbn@mofe.gov.bn | OCP: ocp.mofe.gov.bn | MoFE: mofe.gov.bn

indicators.action: BDCB to review credit policies for SMEs; MoFE + BDCB to establish SME Credit Guarantee Scheme; develop capital market alternatives (Brunei bond market, equity crowdfunding); ensure IFS reporting accuracy; BDCB Governor to engage World Bank on data accuracy.

Data accessed via World Bank WDI portal but sourced from IMF. Rank 76. CRITICAL -20 point decline (35.42 → 15.42). Singapore: 148.5 (rank 3). Brunei's conservative banking sector means SMEs have limited credit access. SME Credit Guarantee Scheme and capital market development are structural fixes. Data reporting accuracy also needs BDCB audit.

indicators.provider: QS Global MBA and Business Masters Rankings 2025
indicators.mechanism: QS Global MBA Rankings evaluate business schools on employer reputation, alumni outcomes, thought leadership, class diversity; minimum requirement: AACSB or EQUIS accreditation OR equivalent quality threshold; Brunei has NO ranked MBA programme
indicators.submission: Business schools apply directly to QS (businessschools@qs.com); requires: AACSB or EQUIS accreditation, minimum 3 years MBA operation, employment outcomes data publication; annual data submission with June deadline; UBDSBE must begin AACSB journey immediately
indicators.included: NO — No Brunei business school in QS MBA Rankings; UBDSBE not AACSB accredited
indicators.eligibility: NOT ELIGIBLE YET. Score=0 is GTCI normalisation floor (Brunei ranked 64/135 globally — it IS scored but at lowest bracket). UBDSBE must obtain AACSB accreditation as prerequisite. 3–5 year accreditation journey. Joint MBA with NUS or Melbourne Business School accelerates pathway.
indicators.action: UBDSBE: submit AACSB Eligibility Application (accreditation@aacsb.edu, budget BND 200,000); pursue joint MBA with NUS Business School (mba.admissions@nus.edu.sg) or Melbourne Business School (mbs-grad@unimelb.edu.au); build research output (30 ABS-3 papers/year); publish annual MBA Employment Report.
indicators.contact: AACSB: accreditation@aacsb.edu | QS MBA: businessschools@qs.com | FT Rankings: ftranking@ft.com | NUS MBA: mba.admissions@nus.edu.sg | Melbourne MBA: mbs-grad@unimelb.edu.au
indicators.insight: Score=0 is GTCI normalisation floor — Brunei IS ranked (64/135) but lowest bracket. AACSB accreditation is the 3–5 year prerequisite for QS ranking consideration. Joint MBA with NUS or Melbourne significantly accelerates the pathway. Annual MBA Employment Report is mandatory QS data requirement.

indicators.name: University Rankings (QS/THE)
indicators.provider: Global Innovation Index Database, WIPO 2025. QS Quacquarelli Symonds Ltd, QS World University Rankings (https://www.topuniversities.com/world-university-rankings/2024). Data Year: 2024
indicators.mechanism: QS and THE collect university data annually: faculty count, student:staff ratio, international faculty/students, citations per faculty, research income, employer reputation, academic reputation survey of 130,000+ academics globally
indicators.submission: UBD and UTB submit data directly to QS (data@qs.com, deadline ~June) and THE (data@timeshighereducation.com, deadline ~July) annually; academic reputation drives ~40% of QS score — requires global academic network engagement
indicators.included: YES — UBD and UTB submit to QS and THE; UBD QS 2026: rank 367 (up 18); THE 2026: UBD rank 351; UTB entered THE (601+)
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. UBD QS trajectory improving. THE: UBD 351, UTB 601+. Key drivers: citations (Central APC Fund BND 500K/yr), international faculty (50 over 3 years), annual QS/THE data quality audit.
indicators.action: Annual QS/THE data audit (QS June, THE July deadlines); Central APC Fund BND 500,000/year for open-access citations; recruit 50 international UBD faculty with h-index >10; UTB THE 401–500 target 2028; UBD VC academic reputation campaign to 500 peer VCs globally.
indicators.contact: QS Rankings: data@qs.com | THE Rankings: data@timeshighereducation.com | UBD Research: research.office@ubd.edu.bn | UTB Research: researchoffice@utb.edu.bn
indicators.insight: ⚠️ GROUP CORRECTED: Was listed under Grp C (UNESCO UIS). Correct group is Grp H (QS/THE Rankings). UBD QS 367 (up 18). THE 351. UTB entered THE. Trajectory positive. Central APC Fund (BND 500K/yr) is highest-citation-impact intervention. International faculty with established citation records transforms both QS and THE scores.

label: GROUP I: Other Agencies
context: 17 indicators | Mixed sources: ITU=AITI submits; WHO=MOH submits; SPI=NOT included (request PMO); Gallup=inconsistent (DEPS survey needed); FSI=automatic; ILO SSI=TAP; Statista=commercial estimate; Yale EPI=automatic | Multiple easy-fix inclusions available

indicators.name: Population Covered by ≥3G Mobile Network
indicators.provider: ITU DataHub — ICT Development Index (IDI)
indicators.mechanism: ITU (International Telecommunication Union) collects mobile network coverage data from national telecom regulatory authorities; AITI collects from DST and Progresif mobile operators and submits to ITU annually
indicators.submission: AITI submits to ITU World Telecommunication/ICT Indicators Database (itustats@itu.int) via annual ITU questionnaire; deadline: January each year; AITI collects coverage data from DST (dst.com.bn) and Progresif (progresif.com)
indicators.included: YES — AITI submits to ITU annually; Score 93.09 (rank 64)
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Score 93.09 — strong. AITI annual ITU submission pipeline functioning correctly. Improve by extending 4G/5G coverage to rural Temburong and Belait interior; include school connectivity data in ITU submission.
indicators.action: AITI to maintain annual ITU questionnaire submission (January deadline); extend 4G/5G to 100% population by 2026 (Temburong, rural Belait/Tutong); MOE to provide school connectivity data to AITI for ITU submission; engage ITU for Brunei Digital Economy Review recognition.
indicators.contact: ITU Statistics: itustats@itu.int | ITU DataHub: datahub.itu.int | AITI: aiti.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Score 93.09 (rank 64). AITI-ITU pipeline functioning well. Extending rural 4G/5G to 100% by 2026 (Temburong Brunei-Muara Bridge corridor) could push score to 96–98. Annual ITU submission maintenance is critical.

indicators.name: Digital Skills
indicators.provider: ITU DataHub — ICT Development Index (IDI)
indicators.mechanism: ITU DataHub digital skills measure based on ITU IDI sub-components; Brunei scores 100 (rank 1) which appears anomalous and contradicts other digital indicators (Cloud=4.79, Enterprise Software=11.86, Software Dev=3.02)
indicators.submission: AITI submits annual ITU questionnaire; score of 100/rank 1 may reflect a specific ITU IDI sub-component where Brunei achieves maximum (possibly internet subscription rate combined with government ICT education programme coverage rates)
indicators.included: YES — AITI submits to ITU; but score appears anomalous — possible GTCI normalisation artefact
indicators.eligibility: PARTICIPATING. Score 100/rank 1 is almost certainly a normalisation artefact in GTCI methodology — likely one specific ITU sub-indicator where Brunei achieves maximum triggers a 100 normalised score. AITI should investigate with ITU and INSEAD GTCI team.
indicators.action: AITI to contact INSEAD GTCI team (gtci@insead.edu) for explanation of 100 score and the specific ITU IDI sub-component; contact ITU Statistics (itustats@itu.int) for Brunei IDI component breakdown; maintain actual ITU submission quality regardless.
indicators.contact: ITU DataHub: datahub.itu.int | ITU Statistics: itustats@itu.int | AITI: aiti.gov.bn | INSEAD/Portulans Institute GTCI: gtci@insead.edu
indicators.insight: Rank 1 globally — but almost certainly a normalisation artefact. Contradicts Enterprise Software (rank 97), Cloud (rank 122), and Software Development (rank 96) scores. AITI must investigate which specific ITU IDI sub-component generates this. Do not over-rely on this score.

indicators.name: Environmental Performance
indicators.provider: Yale Center for Environmental Law & Policy — Environmental Performance Index (EPI) 2024
indicators.mechanism: Yale EPI measures 40 environmental metrics across 11 categories (air quality, water, biodiversity, climate, ecosystem vitality) from WHO, FAO, UNEP, satellite imagery, IEA, and other publicly available international environmental databases
indicators.submission: No direct submission to Yale EPI — data sourced from WHO (air quality), UNEP (ecosystem data), FAO (land use), IEA (energy), satellite imagery; countries improve by actual environmental performance AND ensuring data submitted to underlying source organisations
indicators.included: YES — Brunei automatically included in Yale EPI 2024
indicators.eligibility: AUTOMATICALLY INCLUDED. Improving +2.88 pts. Three-track approach: (1) ensure Brunei environmental data submitted to WHO/UNEP/FAO, (2) actual environmental improvements (Paris Agreement commitments, REDD+ forest carbon), (3) contact Yale EPI team (epi@yale.edu) for methodology consultation.
indicators.action: Ministry of Development (DEPD) to ensure environmental data submitted to WHO, UNEP, FAO, IEA annually; contact Yale EPI team (epi@yale.edu) for Brunei data quality review and missing indicator identification; implement Paris Agreement NDC commitments; REDD+ forest carbon programme.
indicators.contact: Yale EPI: epi@yale.edu | epi.yale.edu | UNEP: unep.org | Ministry of Development: mod.gov.bn | DEPD: depd.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 55, improving +2.88. Yale EPI uses satellite data + international database submissions. Ensure Brunei submits to UNEP and FAO data portals. Brunei's 72% forest cover and zero deforestation policy are major strengths that should register positively in EPI ecosystem sub-scores.

indicators.name: Physician Density
indicators.provider: WHO Global Health Workforce Statistics (National Health Workforce Accounts — NHWA)
indicators.mechanism: WHO NHWA collects physician and health worker density from Ministry of Health annual submissions; derived from health workforce headcount per 10,000 population
indicators.submission: MOH submits annual health workforce data to WHO NHWA portal (apps.who.int/nhwaportal); data includes physicians (all types), nurses, dentists, pharmacists per 10,000 population; ensure BOTH public AND private sector physicians counted in submission
indicators.included: YES — MOH submits to WHO NHWA
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Score reflects actual physician density (~1.5–2.0 per 1,000 population). Improvement: expand PAPRSB-IHS medical school intake, recruit foreign specialists, ensure ALL physicians (public + private + visiting) captured in WHO NHWA submission.
indicators.action: MOH to ensure WHO NHWA submission includes ALL physicians: RIPAS doctors + private clinic doctors + visiting specialists + BNPG military doctors; PAPRSB-IHS (MOE/UBD) to expand medical student intake by 50% by 2028; streamline Medical Council registration for foreign specialists.
indicators.contact: WHO NHWA portal: apps.who.int/nhwaportal | WHO Statistics: nhwa@who.int | MOH: moh.gov.bn | PAPRSB-IHS: fmhs.ubd.edu.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 77. Actual physician density is moderate (~1.5–2.0/1,000). PAPRSB-IHS medical school expansion is the structural fix. Near-term: ensure all physicians (RIPAS, private clinics, visiting specialists) captured in WHO NHWA submission — potential under-reporting.

indicators.name: Sanitation
indicators.provider: WHO/UNICEF Joint Monitoring Programme (JMP) for Water Supply, Sanitation & Hygiene
indicators.mechanism: WHO/UNICEF JMP collects sanitation coverage data from national household surveys, census, and MOH administrative data; Brunei Census 2021 and household surveys provide the basis
indicators.submission: MOH / Department of Public Works (JKR) to submit sanitation coverage data to WHO/UNICEF JMP (washdata.org; jmp@who.int); census data and household surveys provide the evidence base; next update should reflect Census 2021 data
indicators.included: YES — Brunei included in WHO/UNICEF JMP; Score 98 (rank 43)
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Score 98/100 — near-perfect. Ensure rural Temburong and remote Belait sanitation coverage included in JMP submission. Target 100% to match Singapore by GTCI 2027.
indicators.action: MOH to ensure WHO/UNICEF JMP submission includes Temburong and all rural districts; Ministry of Development (JKR) to extend sanitation infrastructure to remaining 2% of population; annual JMP data submission with Census 2021 data baseline.
indicators.contact: WHO/UNICEF JMP: washdata.org | jmp@who.int | MOH: moh.gov.bn | Ministry of Development: mod.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Score 98 (rank 43). Near-universal sanitation. Close remaining 2% gap through Temburong and rural infrastructure. Singapore = 100 (rank 1). Brunei can achieve 100 by 2027 with targeted Temburong sanitation infrastructure investment.

indicators.name: Pension Coverage
indicators.provider: ILO Social Security Inquiry / UN SDG Indicators Database (SDG 1.3.1)
indicators.mechanism: ILO Social Security Inquiry collects social protection coverage statistics from national social security institutions; Brunei TAP (Tabung Amanah Pekerja) coverage data submitted by TAP/MoFE to ILO annually
indicators.submission: TAP and MoFE submit social security coverage statistics to ILO Social Security Inquiry (socpro@ilo.org); annual submission; data also submitted to UN SDG 1.3.1 monitoring framework (unstats.un.org/sdgs)
indicators.included: YES — WORLD RANK 1; 100% universal pension coverage
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & PARTICIPATING. World Rank 1 — 100% pension coverage through TAP. MAINTAIN: ensure annual TAP coverage data submitted to ILO Social Security Inquiry on time; leverage world rank 1 in all GTCI communications.
indicators.action: MAINTAIN: TAP to submit annual coverage data to ILO Social Security Inquiry (socpro@ilo.org); MoFE to communicate world rank 1 in all talent retention and attraction marketing; leverage this exceptional strength in ASEAN talent competition messaging.
indicators.contact: ILO Social Security Inquiry: socpro@ilo.org | TAP: tap.com.bn | ILO SOCPRO: ilo.org/socpro
indicators.insight: WORLD RANK 1 — universal pension coverage (alongside Singapore at 100). Exceptional strength. TAP = 100% formal workforce coverage. Leverage in all talent retention communications. A unique global differentiator for Brunei.

indicators.name: Personal Rights
indicators.provider: Social Progress Imperative (SPI) — Social Progress Index
indicators.mechanism: SPI compiles Social Progress Index from 57 indicators across 3 dimensions (Basic Human Needs, Foundations of Wellbeing, Opportunity) sourced from WHO, UNICEF, UNDP, ITU, Freedom House, WEF; Brunei NOT currently included in SPI; two-for-one opportunity with 4.2.2
indicators.submission: SPI can be engaged to include Brunei at results@socialprogressimperative.org; SPI uses publicly available international data but requires minimum data coverage threshold; PMO should lead engagement with detailed Brunei data package
indicators.included: NO — Brunei not included in Social Progress Index
indicators.eligibility: NOT INCLUDED. TWO-FOR-ONE with 4.2.2. ONE SPI engagement request fixes BOTH 4.2.1 and 4.2.2 simultaneously. PMO/AGC to contact Social Progress Imperative with evidence package (governance data, RBPF crime data, WHO health data, property rights framework).
indicators.action: PMO/AGC to contact Social Progress Imperative (results@socialprogressimperative.org) with full Brunei evidence package; provide data for all SPI indicator categories; ensure underlying WHO, UNDP, Freedom House data sources cover Brunei; one request fixes 4.2.1 AND 4.2.2.
indicators.agency: Prime Minister's Office / Attorney General's Chambers
indicators.contact: Social Progress Imperative: results@socialprogressimperative.org | socialprogressimperative.org | PMO: pmo.gov.bn | AGC: agc.gov.bn
indicators.insight: TWO-FOR-ONE: one SPI request fixes 4.2.1 AND 4.2.2 simultaneously. PMO should lead. While Brunei's political rights score may be moderate, personal liberties (property rights, freedom of movement, freedom of religion) are respected. Expected score: 40–60/100.

indicators.name: Personal Safety
indicators.provider: Social Progress Imperative (SPI) — Social Progress Index
indicators.mechanism: Same SPI source as 4.2.1 — Personal Safety measures violent crime, road accidents, and perceived safety; Brunei's actual crime rate is among the lowest in ASEAN; RBPF statistics support an excellent score
indicators.submission: Same SPI engagement mechanism as 4.2.1; one SPI engagement request resolves BOTH 4.2.1 and 4.2.2; RBPF annual crime statistics provide the evidence base for a very high personal safety score
indicators.included: NO — Brunei not included in Social Progress Index
indicators.eligibility: NOT INCLUDED. TWO-FOR-ONE with 4.2.1. Brunei's actual personal safety is exceptional (one of lowest crime rates in ASEAN). Expected score: 70–85/100. RBPF annual crime reports provide strong evidence base. ONE SPI request = TWO indicators resolved.
indicators.action: Ministry of Home Affairs / RBPF to provide crime statistics to PMO for SPI evidence package; PMO to contact Social Progress Imperative (results@socialprogressimperative.org) with RBPF crime data, WHO road safety data, and DOSH workplace safety statistics; one request fixes 4.2.1 AND 4.2.2.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / RBPF / PMO
indicators.contact: Social Progress Imperative: results@socialprogressimperative.org | RBPF: police.gov.bn | MOH: moh.gov.bn
indicators.insight: TWO-FOR-ONE with 4.2.1. Brunei has one of the LOWEST crime rates in Southeast Asia. RBPF crime statistics (assault, theft, homicide, violent crime) would support an excellent score of 70–85/100. Single SPI engagement resolves both indicators. RBPF data package can be assembled within weeks.

indicators.name: Protect Against Future Disasters
indicators.provider: Lloyd's Register Foundation World Risk Poll (WRP) 2023
indicators.mechanism: World Risk Poll surveys ~125,000 people in 142 countries on perceptions of risks and safety; fieldwork conducted by Gallup on behalf of Lloyd's Register Foundation (LRF); Brunei not currently surveyed
indicators.submission: Countries cannot directly request WRP inclusion — LRF and Gallup determine coverage; formal engagement with LRF (info@lrfoundation.org.uk) can advocate for Brunei inclusion in WRP 2027; DOSH workplace safety data provides evidence of Brunei's actual safety record
indicators.included: NO — Brunei not in World Risk Poll
indicators.eligibility: NOT SURVEYED. Formal engagement needed with Lloyd's Register Foundation for WRP 2027 inclusion. Brunei's disaster risk profile is genuinely low (minimal seismic, no typhoon zone, low flood risk; strong NDMC). Expected score: 60–80/100. DOSH safety data is the evidence base.
indicators.action: Ministry of Home Affairs (NDMC) to contact Lloyd's Register Foundation (info@lrfoundation.org.uk) requesting Brunei inclusion in WRP 2027; provide DOSH workplace safety statistics, NDMC disaster management capacity data, and Brunei hazard risk profile as supporting evidence.
indicators.agency: Ministry of Home Affairs / NDMC / DOSH
indicators.contact: Lloyd's Register Foundation: info@lrfoundation.org.uk | World Risk Poll: wrp.lrfoundation.org.uk | NDMC: moha.gov.bn | DOSH: labour.gov.bn/dosh
indicators.insight: Brunei empirically safe from natural disasters (minimal seismic, no major typhoon/flood risk). NDMC + DOSH data provides strong evidence. LRF/Gallup engagement for WRP 2027 inclusion is the path. Expected score 60–80 when included. Act now for 2027 cycle planning.

indicators.name: Employee Wellbeing
indicators.provider: Gallup World Poll — Wellbeing Index
indicators.mechanism: Gallup World Poll annually surveys ~1,000 adults per country on 100+ topics including employment satisfaction and life wellbeing; Brunei's Gallup coverage is inconsistent — not consistently included in every wave
indicators.submission: Gallup selects countries for annual World Poll; formal inclusion requires Gallup partnership; DEPS can commission Gallup-compatible national wellbeing survey and share results with INSEAD GTCI; cost: BND 50,000–100,000/year for national wellbeing survey
indicators.included: PARTIAL — Brunei's Gallup coverage is inconsistent; wellbeing score not available
indicators.eligibility: PARTIALLY COVERED BUT INCONSISTENT. Two approaches: (1) Commission annual National Wellbeing Survey using Gallup-compatible methodology (BND 50–100K/year), publish results, share with INSEAD GTCI; (2) Contact Gallup (globalresearch@gallup.com) for consistent World Poll Brunei coverage.
indicators.action: DEPS to commission annual National Wellbeing Survey (Gallup-compatible methodology, BND 50–100K/year); contact Gallup (globalresearch@gallup.com) for consistent Brunei World Poll coverage; share published results with INSEAD GTCI (gtci@insead.edu) for GTCI 2027 data cycle.
indicators.agency: DEPS / Ministry of Finance & Economy / MOH
indicators.contact: Gallup World Poll: globalresearch@gallup.com | gallup.com/analytics/232838/world-poll.aspx | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | INSEAD/Portulans Institute GTCI: gtci@insead.edu
indicators.insight: Brunei's actual employee wellbeing is likely high (job security, free healthcare, no income tax, zero violent crime, stable society). But not captured in Gallup data. National Wellbeing Survey (BND 50–100K/year) using Gallup methodology + GTCI team engagement = path to inclusion in GTCI 2027.

indicators.name: Tolerance of Immigrants
indicators.provider: Gallup World Poll — Tolerance Survey
indicators.mechanism: Gallup World Poll tolerance question on whether respondents' city/area is a good place for immigrants; Brunei has ~31% migrant population (one of ASEAN's highest shares) but not consistently in Gallup annual survey
indicators.submission: Same Gallup World Poll mechanism as 4.2.5; DEPS to request consistent Gallup Brunei coverage; alternatively commission Gallup-compatible national perception survey; Brunei's 31% migrant population demonstrates actual tolerance
indicators.included: PARTIAL — Brunei Gallup coverage inconsistent; tolerance score not available
indicators.eligibility: NOT CONSISTENTLY COVERED. Brunei's actual immigrant tolerance is demonstrated by ~31% migrant population. DEPS to commission Gallup-compatible annual household survey including tolerance questions; alternatively engage Gallup (globalresearch@gallup.com) for consistent coverage.
indicators.action: DEPS to include Gallup-compatible tolerance/immigration questions in annual household survey; contact Gallup (globalresearch@gallup.com) for consistent Brunei World Poll coverage; share results with INSEAD GTCI; MoHA to communicate Brunei's inclusive migrant worker policies.
indicators.contact: Gallup: globalresearch@gallup.com | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn | Ministry of Home Affairs: moha.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Brunei has ~31% migrant population — empirically one of most tolerant toward immigrants in ASEAN. But Gallup coverage inconsistent. DEPS national survey with Gallup-compatible questions + GTCI team engagement = path to inclusion. Expected score 40–60 based on observable tolerance levels.

indicators.name: Tolerance of Minorities
indicators.provider: Fund for Peace Fragile States Index (FSI) 2024
indicators.mechanism: FSI measures state fragility using Group Grievance and Social Cohesion indicators from automated media analysis (CAST methodology), expert surveys, and national statistics; Brunei automatically included as sovereign state
indicators.submission: Fund for Peace compiles FSI from automated media monitoring, expert surveys, and quantitative data; no direct national submission; improvement requires actual policy changes, positive social cohesion programmes, AND media narrative management
indicators.included: YES — Automatically included via FSI; Rank 93 — significant weakness
indicators.eligibility: AUTOMATICALLY INCLUDED. Rank 93 — one of Brunei's weakest GTCI indicators. FSI Group Grievance sub-indicator is driven by perceptions of minority exclusion. Engagement with Fund for Peace + social cohesion programmes + multi-religious harmony narrative are the improvement pathways.
indicators.action: PMO to engage Fund for Peace (info@fundforpeace.org) for methodology discussion and Brunei narrative correction; review social cohesion policies; communicate Brunei's multi-religious harmony and national identity initiatives; National SDG office to submit social cohesion data; legal review of minority rights framework.
indicators.agency: Prime Minister's Office / Ministry of Culture, Youth & Sports
indicators.contact: Fund for Peace FSI: fragilestatessindex.org | info@fundforpeace.org | PMO: pmo.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 93 — significant weakness. FSI Group Grievance metric reflects perceptions captured through automated media monitoring. Driven by international coverage of SPC enforcement and MIB state religion. Long-term: social cohesion programmes, positive international narrative on religious harmony, and Fund for Peace direct engagement.

indicators.name: Enterprise Software
indicators.provider: Statista Technology Market Outlook / World Bank WDI (normalisation)
indicators.mechanism: Statista derives enterprise software market size per capita from commercial market research (vendor revenues, market surveys, CIO surveys); World Bank WDI provides GDP normalisation; Brunei enterprise software spend estimated from market intelligence — no direct government survey
indicators.submission: No direct data submission pathway — Statista derives from commercial market intelligence (vendor revenue reporting, regional CIO surveys, market research); improvement requires actual increase in enterprise software adoption across Brunei businesses and government agencies
indicators.included: AUTO-INCLUDED via commercial market intelligence estimate — Brunei market size estimated by Statista from regional data; score may under-capture actual enterprise software spend in O&G sector
indicators.eligibility: AUTOMATICALLY ESTIMATED. No direct submission pathway. Structural fix required: national digitalisation mandate, SME software adoption subsidies, Cloud-First Policy, GLC ERP mandates. Score 11.86 reflects genuinely low adoption (rank 97/135).
indicators.action: MTIC/AITI: implement Digital-First mandate for all government agencies; DE to provide enterprise software adoption grants (BND 5,000 per SME, 500 SMEs/year); AITI Digital Transformation Programme acceleration; mandate ERP for all GLCs and Ministries; engage Statista (info@statista.com) on Brunei market data accuracy.
indicators.contact: Statista: info@statista.com | statista.com/outlook/tmo/software | MTIC: mtic.gov.bn | AITI: aiti.gov.bn | DE: de.gov.bn
indicators.insight: Rank 97. Score 11.86 — genuinely low enterprise software adoption. Singapore = 64.21 (gap: 52.3 pts). SME digitalisation grants and government Cloud-First Policy are the key structural fixes. AITI Enterprise Digital Transformation programme needs significant acceleration.

indicators.name: Cloud Computing
indicators.provider: Statista Technology Market Outlook / World Bank WDI (normalisation)
indicators.mechanism: Statista derives public cloud market size per capita from commercial vendor revenue data (AWS, Azure, GCP, Alibaba Cloud revenue in Brunei), market research, and CIO surveys; World Bank WDI provides GDP normalisation
indicators.submission: No direct data submission — Statista commercial market intelligence from cloud vendor revenues; improvement requires actual increase in cloud adoption by government, enterprises, and SMEs in Brunei; engage hyperscalers for Brunei data presence
indicators.included: AUTO-INCLUDED via commercial cloud market revenue estimates — Brunei cloud market size estimated from regional vendor revenue data
indicators.eligibility: AUTOMATICALLY ESTIMATED. Score 4.79 (rank 122) reflects ACTUAL very low cloud adoption. No direct submission pathway. Structural fixes: Cloud-First Policy mandate, sovereign cloud framework, SME cloud adoption grants, hyperscaler (AWS/Azure/GCP) Brunei data centre or cloud region investment.
indicators.action: MTIC/AITI: Cloud-First mandate for all government non-sensitive workloads; SME cloud adoption grants (BND 3,000 per SME); sovereign cloud framework under AITI; AITI cloud skills certification; engage AWS (aws.amazon.com/contact-us), Azure (azure.microsoft.com), GCP for Brunei cloud region or local zone investment.
indicators.contact: MTIC: mtic.gov.bn | AITI: aiti.gov.bn | DE25 Digital Economy Masterplan: mtic.gov.bn | AWS: aws.amazon.com | Azure: azure.microsoft.com
indicators.insight: Rank 122 — CRITICAL weakness. Score 4.79. Singapore = 68.54 (gap: 63.7 pts). Genuine structural gap. Cloud-First Policy is overdue. Engaging AWS, Azure, or GCP for a Brunei cloud region or local zone would transform cloud adoption metrics. DE25 Digital Economy Masterplan must explicitly target this.

indicators.name: Labour Productivity per Employee
indicators.provider: The Conference Board, Total Economy Database (TED) — www.conference-board.org/data/economydatabase
indicators.mechanism: Conference Board computes GDP per employed person using national accounts + labour force data; Brunei NOT currently in TED database
indicators.submission: DEPS to contact Conference Board (economics@conference-board.org) and submit: GDP time series (constant prices) + total employment series. All data exists at DEPS already. This is a pure data provision exercise — no surveys needed.
indicators.included: NO — Brunei not in Conference Board TED database
indicators.eligibility: NOT YET PARTICIPATING. Eligible as sovereign nation. DEPS must contact Conference Board directly. All required data (GDP and employment) already published by DEPS. One email + data file = indicator resolved.
indicators.action: DEPS to email economics@conference-board.org; provide annual GDP (constant BND) series and LFS employment totals; request TED inclusion for historical and forward data; straightforward data provision exercise.
indicators.contact: The Conference Board: economics@conference-board.org | TED portal: conference-board.org/data/economydatabase | DEPS: deps.mofe.gov.bn
indicators.insight: ⚠️ GROUP CORRECTED: Source is Conference Board TED, not ILOSTAT. Belongs to GRP I (Other Int'l Agencies). CRITICAL EASY FIX. DEPS has all required data (GDP series + LFS employment). Simply contact Conference Board. Score improvement: N/A → 40–60/100. Could add 0.3–0.5 to Brunei's VTS sub-pillar score immediately.

indicators.name: Household Financial Resilience
indicators.provider: World Risk Poll Resilience Index — Lloyd's Register Foundation / Gallup (https://www.lrfoundation.org.uk/wrp/world-risk-poll-data/world-risk-poll-resilience-index)
indicators.mechanism: World Bank Global Findex surveys financial inclusion and resilience in ~150 countries every 3 years; Gallup conducts fieldwork on behalf of World Bank; Brunei not included in Global Findex 2021 or 2024
indicators.submission: Countries REQUEST World Bank to include them in Global Findex; BDCB has comprehensive national financial inclusion data showing >90% bank account ownership; next Findex cycle: 2027; MoFE must request NOW to be included in 2027
indicators.included: NO — Brunei not in Global Findex 2024
indicators.eligibility: NOT PARTICIPATING. BDCB must request World Bank to include Brunei in Global Findex 2027. Brunei's actual financial inclusion is strong (>90% bank accounts, universal TAP coverage). Request should be made by MoFE to World Bank Findex team NOW for planning.
indicators.action: BDCB/MoFE to contact World Bank Global Findex team (findex@worldbank.org) requesting Brunei inclusion in Global Findex 2027; submit BDCB national financial inclusion data as interim evidence; engage Gallup (globalresearch@gallup.com) as Findex fieldwork partner for Brunei.
indicators.contact: World Bank Global Findex: findex@worldbank.org | Findex portal: worldbank.org/en/publication/globalfindex | BDCB: bdcb.gov.bn
indicators.insight: ⚠️ SOURCE CORRECTED: Was incorrectly listed as Global Findex Database (World Bank/CGAP). PDF Appendix III confirms: World Risk Poll Resilience Index by Lloyd's Register Foundation. Brunei's financial inclusion is genuinely strong (>90% bank accounts). BDCB data exists. Issue: not in Global Findex. MoFE must formally request World Bank inclusion for Findex 2027. Expected score: 70–85/100 when included. Act now for 2027 cycle.

indicators.name: Use of Virtual Social Networks
indicators.provider: We Are Social and Hootsuite (2025) Digital 2025 report series (https://wearesocial.com/digital-2025)
indicators.mechanism: EOS question on the use of virtual social networks for business collaboration and professional purposes
indicators.submission: WEF EOS via UTB NPI; annual online survey to ~100 Brunei executives
indicators.included: YES — Brunei participates via UTB NPI
indicators.eligibility: ELIGIBLE & ACTIVELY PARTICIPATING. Moderate score. Improvement requires growing actual professional digital platform use in Brunei businesses AND briefing EOS respondents on digital business ecosystem.
indicators.action: MTIC/AITI to promote professional digital platform adoption among Brunei businesses; UTB to brief EOS respondents on growing tech ecosystem (e-commerce, digital collaboration, BruHack); DE Digital Transformation programme acceleration.
indicators.contact: WEF EOS: eos@weforum.org | UTB (NPI): cco.international@utb.edu.bn | AITI: aiti.gov.bn
indicators.insight: ⚠️ SOURCE CORRECTED: Was wrongly listed as WEF EOS. PDF confirms: We Are Social & Hootsuite Digital 2025. Move to GRP I (Other Int'l Agencies). Rank 70. Brunei has high internet penetration but professional social network use for business not yet deeply embedded. UTB EOS briefing on growing DE digital ecosystem and BruHack innovation community will help shift perceptions.
